name: W9 Legs Morning Flow (QL Release)
description: 15 min. QL/hip flexor release focused — this is the day to be generous with mobility. Lacrosse ball QL release, long hip flexor stretches, gentle spinal decompression. Goes before the deloaded legs session.

exercises.notes: 60s. Decompress the low back to start.
60s each side. Gentle twist — exactly what the QL wants. Don't force, let gravity do it.
90s each side (longer than usual). The QL and psoas share fascial connections — pigeon gets both.
60s each side. Deep hip flexor stretch — this is exactly the area that was seizing on W8D5.
60s each side. The most direct quad + hip flexor stretch. Wall or couch — knee bent, opposite foot up behind.
Slow and breathing. Feel for any remaining QL tightness.
Gentle squeeze — wake up glutes without bracing hard. Glutes inhibited = QL overworks, so this actually helps.
SLOW. Gentle core engagement, no crunching. 8 reps, not 10.

tips: RECOVERY FLOW — longer holds, no activation-heavy work.
Pigeon for 90s each side — the longer hold lets the QL/psoas actually let go.
If there's a lacrosse ball at the gym, roll the QL before coming in — both sides of the low back, above the iliac crest.
Skip glute bridge + dead bug if anything feels twitchy. The stretches are the whole workout if needed.
Pigeon, child's pose, and supine twist are the three 'if I only have time for three' moves this week.
